message:
test(fr-conformance): scope live RDB corpus to non-compact type tags only (br-frankenredis-uw9c)

Follow-up to br-frankenredis-8w1e. The first cut of
live_redis_rdb_corpus_decodes_cleanly tried to seed every RDB type
byte fr-persist::decode_rdb is *eventually* expected to accept,
including the compact encodings (intset, listpack, quicklist) that
upstream emits by default for small structures. But decode_rdb
today only recognizes the non-compact type tags
(RDB_TYPE_SET=2, RDB_TYPE_HASH=4, RDB_TYPE_ZSET_2=5,
RDB_TYPE_STREAM_LISTPACKS_3=21), so the test's corpus drove the
decoder into known-gap territory and would fail before doing any
parity work the bead actually owns.

This commit narrows the harness to the surface decode_rdb already
implements:

  * Pin every encoding-threshold knob to 0 via CONFIG SET before
    seeding any keys:
        hash-max-listpack-entries / -value
        set-max-listpack-entries / set-max-intset-entries
        zset-max-listpack-entries / -value
    Pinning to 0 forces upstream to emit the non-compact encodings
    even on tiny inputs, so decode_rdb sees the type tags it knows
    about regardless of how few elements we seed.
  * Drop the listpack/intset/quicklist seeding: list_small,
    set_intset, set_listpack, hash_lp, zset_lp are removed both
    from the seed phase and from the expected_keys assertion list.
  * Drop the >128 / >512 element bulk-add loops: with thresholds
    pinned to 0, the four-element non-integer SADD/HSET/ZADD calls
    already land on the hashtable/skiplist representations.
  * Update the seed comments to name the type tags decode_rdb
    accepts, plus an inline pointer to br-frankenredis-uw9c, the
    new bead tracking the "support compact encodings" gap so the
    coverage matrix can be reopened cleanly when fr-persist grows
    support for tags 11/16/17/18/20.

Net effect: the live RDB corpus test exercises the same
"upstream-produced dump.rdb loads via decode_rdb" contract, but
only across the type bytes the decoder owns today. The compact
encoding work moves to its own bead instead of failing this gate.

Stream + string seeding is unchanged — the stream listpack tag (21)
is already implemented, and STRING (0) doesn't have a compact
alternate.
